'''''Dreadnaught-Three''''', known as '''''D-Three''''' for short, was the third of six [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] used on ''[[Outbound Flight]]''.

==History==
It came directly off the [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] line for use on the project in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. After ''Outbound Flight'' crashed on [[The Redoubt/Legends|the Redoubt]], ''Dreadnaught-Three'' was used as a place to exile [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]], due to its isolation. As of [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], that had only been done twice.

The ship was anomalous, in that it had no survivors after the crash, and contained a [[Delta-12 Skysprite-class|Delta-12 ''Skysprite'']], originally intended to carry [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] back to [[Galactic Republic/Legends|the Republic]] before Outbound Flight left [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were originally aboard under [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] orders to spy on [[Jorus C'baoth]] after his behavior became more and more erratic. They were eventually called off the ship at its last stop in Republic space by [[Palpatine/Legends|Supreme Chancellor Palpatine]]. Thus, the Skysprite was never utilized. [[Jorad Pressor]]'s father disassembled the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] of the ship to prevent its [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] captives from escaping.
